title: Why Distilled Models Run Faster
concept: The student model has far fewer layers and parameters, requiring significantly less memory and compute per token during inference.

exercises.prompt: What is the main goal of model distillation?

options.text: To make the model smaller and faster.
To make the model take up more hard drive space.
To train the model on how to write code faster.

exercises.explain: Knowledge distillation transfers knowledge from a large, slow 'teacher' model to a smaller, faster 'student' model.

cards.title: Instant Answers
cards.body: Have you ever wondered how some AI chatbots reply instantly, while others take several seconds to think? The secret isn't a faster internet connection; it's that the fast AI has undergone 'distillation' to become smaller and speedier!

cards.title: Fewer Layers, Faster Speed
cards.body: Distilled models run faster because they are physically smaller. They have fewer layers (processing steps) and parameters (connections). Because there is less math to do for each word generated, they need less computer memory and can make decisions in a fraction of the time.

cards.title: The Pocket Cheat Sheet
cards.body: Think of a giant, 1,000-page encyclopedia. It has the answer to everything, but carrying it around and turning pages takes a long time. Now, think of a small pocket cheat sheet containing just the key facts. The cheat sheet is much lighter, fits in your pocket, and lets you find the answer in a split second. The giant book is the teacher model; the handy cheat sheet is the student model!

cards.title: AI on Your Phone
cards.body: Imagine running an AI assistant on your phone. A massive teacher model is too big to fit, so it has to send your query to a distant server, taking seconds to reply. A distilled student model is small enough to run directly inside your phone's memory, giving you instant answers even without internet!

cards.title: They Aren't Just Rushing
cards.body: People often think that small, distilled models are fast because they just skip steps or rush through the work. In reality, they are fast because they have a simpler architecture. They do less math overall, but they do it just as carefully as the giant models!

cards.title: Teacher vs. Student Structure
cards.body: Let's compare the structure of the giant teacher model with the streamlined student model to see why the student runs so much faster.

left.text: Hundreds of layers, billions of parameters. Huge memory footprint, slow generation.

right.text: Few layers, millions of parameters. Fits in phone memory, near-instant generation.

exercises.prompt: What does a distilled student model do during inference that makes it faster than the teacher?

options.text: It performs fewer mathematical calculations per token.
It pauses to search the internet for answers.
It deletes the user's prompt to save space.
It uses more computer memory to speed things up.

exercises.explain: During inference (running the model to get an answer), having fewer parameters means the computer performs fewer mathematical operations per token, saving both time and energy.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A distilled student model runs faster because it has fewer parameters to compute than the teacher model.
exercises.explain: Reducing the number of layers and parameters reduces the computational work, allowing the student model to run much faster with less memory.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to explain why distilled models are so fast.
exercises.sentence: Distilled models are faster because they have fewer layers and ______ to process.
exercises.explain: Fewer parameters and layers mean the computer does less math for every word (token) it generates, which makes the model run much faster.

exercises.prompt: Which of these represents a successful distilled model?

options.text: A student model that has the exact same number of layers as the teacher.
A student model with 90% fewer parameters that runs 10 times faster.
A teacher model that gets slower after teaching a student.

exercises.explain: The student model is a streamlined version of the teacher model. It has fewer parameters and layers, which makes it much faster, while the teacher remains giant and slow.

exercises.prompt: Why exactly does a distilled 'student' model generate answers faster than its giant 'teacher' model?

options.text: They use a faster programming language than teacher models.
They have fewer layers and parameters, requiring fewer calculations per token.
They only work on supercomputers, which speeds them up.
They skip the middle words in a prompt to finish faster.

exercises.explain: Distilled models run faster primarily because they are physically smaller. With fewer layers and parameters, they perform fewer math calculations per word they generate, making them highly efficient.
